answer:
**San Hyan Tattvajnana** (Knowledge of Reality) and **San Hyan Mahajnana** (Sacred Supreme Wisdom) are both medieval Old Javanese (*Kawi*) prose treatises on *jñāna* (spiritual knowledge), drawing from Śaiva-Śākta and Sāṅkhya-Yoga frameworks to expound the nature of the *ātmā* (Self), states of consciousness (*avasthā*, e.g., *jāgrat*/waking, *svapna*/dream, *suṣupti*/deep sleep, *tūrya*/fourth), cosmic principles (*tattva*), and paths to *mokṣa* (liberation).[^1][^2] However, they diverge in epistemological approach: *Tattvajnana* is systematic and analytical, emphasizing *pramāṇa* (valid means of knowledge) to dissect reality hierarchically; *Mahajnana* is revelatory and intuitive, conveyed through guru-disciple dialogue, riddles (*praśna*), and direct gnosis (*upadeśa*) of the indwelling Śiva.

### Epistemological Angle of *San Hyan Tattvajnana*
This text adopts a rigorously analytical epistemology, rooted in three *pramāṇas* (*pratyakṣa*/direct perception for coarse phenomena, *anumāna*/inference for subtle states like *tūrya*, and *āgama*/scripture for the subtlest, e.g., *tūryānta*).[^3] It maps knowledge hierarchically: the Divine *Ātmā* (distinguished as active *Pramāṇa* or inactive *Ātmāviśeṣa*/Dharma) animates *citta* (stained by *triguṇa*: *sattva*, *rajas*, *tamas*), yielding *buddhi* (discriminative intellect, aware of good/evil), *ahaṅkāra* (ego, creating senses/*daśendriya* and elements/*mahābhūta*), and transmigration.[^4] Knowledge progresses via *prayogasandhi* (yogic union: *āsana*, *prāṇāyāma*, etc.), burning *vāsanā* (latent tendencies) to exhaust *sattva*-fruit and unite with Paramaśivatattva.[^5] Epistemology is constructive: reality is known by inferring subtle from coarse (e.g., *ātmā* grades cause human wisdom disparities), verified by *samyagjñāna* (perfect knowledge) as lamp.[^6]

### Epistemological Angle of *San Hyan Mahajnana*
In contrast, *Mahajnana* employs a mystical, non-discursive epistemology via *upadeśa* (guru's secret instruction) in Śiva-Kumāra dialogue, using riddles to pierce illusion (*māyā*) and realize Śiva in the body-microcosm (e.g., states/deities: *jāgrat*/Brahmā at navel, *tūrya*/Maheśvara at throat/nose).[^7] Knowledge is immediate gnosis: internal *ātma-liṅga* (Self-symbol) trumps external worship; *oṃkāra* (as boat/fire) burns kleśas (*kāma*, etc.) for *paramakaivalya* (supreme isolation).[^8] No systematic *pramāṇas*; truth is contemplative identity (e.g., body as lotus/channels/*nāḍī*, heart as void-abode), abandoning *vyakta*/*avyakta* (manifest/unmanifest) for quality-less *jñāna*.[^9] Epistemology is deconstructive: riddles reveal pre-existing unity (e.g., *puruṣa* as sun, *prakṛti* as night), attained by grace/guru-service.[^10]

*Tattvajnana* builds knowledge bottom-up (coarse to subtle via *pramāṇa*/yoga); *Mahajnana* collapses dualities top-down (direct Śiva-gnosis via *upadeśa*). Both converge on *tūrya*-realization, but *Tattvajnana* suits systematic yogins, *Mahajnana* intuitive devotees.[^11]

[^1]: *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, ch.1, [full chapter](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/).

[^2]: *San Hyan Mahajnana*, ch.1, [full chapter](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/).

[^3]: *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, ch.1 [p-1da719](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-1da719).

[^4]: *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, ch.1 [p-1792eb](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-1792eb), [p-aea5b2](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-aea5b2).

[^5]: *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, ch.1 [p-a3f73c](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-a3f73c).

[^6]: *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, ch.1 [p-6dc057](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-6dc057).

[^7]: *San Hyan Mahajnana*, ch.1 [p-7777f3](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-7777f3), [p-90111a](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-90111a).

[^8]: *San Hyan Mahajnana*, ch.1 [p-f64c7b](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-f64c7b), [p-0f7a95](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-0f7a95).

[^9]: *San Hyan Mahajnana*, ch.1 [p-2047cf](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-2047cf)–[p-a9e459](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-a9e459).

[^10]: *San Hyan Mahajnana*, ch.1 [p-573696](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-573696), [p-beca2b](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-beca2b).

[^11]: Both nearest to *Vrhaspatitattva* (cosine ~0.5/0.3); mutual similarity ~0.22.[^1][^2]
